purpose: Displays Jira work items on a monthly/weekly calendar grid by due date, lets users schedule/reschedule work via drag-and-drop from an Unscheduled panel, and create new items directly on a date.

verified_against.fixture_card: AUT-1 'Design autonomous replanning loop' (unscheduled, Task, To Do, High priority)

feature_checks.feature: Calendar tab loads under AUT project with Calendar tab marked active
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Navigate to /jira/core/projects/AUT/calendar and assert the Space navigation has a heading level=2 named 'Calendar' (active tab) and siblings link to Summary, Board, List, Timeline, Approvals, Forms, Docs.

feature_checks.feature: Space header shows 'Autoloop' H1 with Share, Automation, Give feedback, Enter full screen controls
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert heading level=1 with name 'Autoloop' is visible, and buttons named 'Share', 'Automation', 'Give feedback', 'Enter full screen' are present in the page header.

feature_checks.feature: Calendar search textbox filters work items on the calendar
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a textbox with accessible name 'Search calendar' is present in the toolbar.

feature_checks.feature: Assignee quick-filter avatar row with a checkbox per assignee
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a group labelled 'Filter by assignee' is present and contains at least one checkbox whose name ends with 'is online' or 'is offline' (e.g. 'Daniel Li is online').

feature_checks.feature: '0 filters applied' button opens a filter popup with full section list
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Click the button with accessible name '0 filters applied' and assert the popup contains these section headings/fields.

feature_checks.feature: 'Today' button resets the calendar view to the current month
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a button with accessible name 'Today' is present in the date-controls region.

feature_checks.feature: Previous/Next month buttons flanking a heading that shows the current month (e.g. 'Apr 2026')
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert buttons named 'Previous month' and 'Next month' exist and a level-3 heading shows a 'MMM YYYY' label (e.g. 'Apr 2026'). Clicking 'Next month' updates the URL to include '?date=YYYY-MM-01' and updates the heading to the next month.
feature_checks.notes: Interaction is URL-level and reversible by clicking Today or the opposite arrow.

feature_checks.feature: 'Month options' dropdown switches between Month and Week views
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Click the button labelled 'Month options' (or the responsive 'Month' trigger) and assert the opened menu contains menuitems 'Month' and 'Week'.

feature_checks.feature: 'Unscheduled work' toggle opens/closes the Unscheduled side panel
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a button named 'Unscheduled work' is present in the toolbar. Click it and assert a complementary region named 'Unscheduled work' appears; click again to close.

feature_checks.feature: 'View settings' popover exposes display preferences
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Click the 'View settings' button and assert the popover contains the labels below.
feature_checks.default_checked: Color by = Status; Week starts on = Sunday

feature_checks.feature: Calendar grid columnheaders for weekdays
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a grid exists and its column headers include 'Monday', 'Tuesday', 'Wednesday', 'Thursday', 'Friday' (weekend columns may be hidden by the 'Show weekends' default).
feature_checks.notes: Saturday/Sunday are hidden by default because 'Show weekends' is off.

feature_checks.feature: Each visible weekday of the current month renders a day gridcell with a numeric label
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert the grid has rowgroups with rows of gridcells whose accessible names match full dates (e.g. 'April 1, 2026'), and each cell shows a day number (e.g. '1', '15', or 'Apr 1' / 'May 1' on month boundaries).

feature_checks.feature: Hovering/focusing a day cell reveals a 'Create work item' button to add an item on that date
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert every visible day gridcell contains a button named 'Create work item'. (Do not submit the inline form — presence only.)
feature_checks.reverted_by: Do not submit; press Escape to dismiss the inline creator if it opens.

feature_checks.feature: Unscheduled work side panel lists items lacking a due date and supports drag-to-schedule
feature_checks.how_to_verify: With the panel open, assert a complementary region named 'Unscheduled work' with a heading level=2 'Unscheduled work', the helper text 'Drag each work item onto the calendar to set a due date for the work.', and a 'Close panel' button.

feature_checks.feature: Unscheduled panel has its own search textbox
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a textbox with placeholder 'Search unscheduled items' (aria-label mentions 'Search field for unscheduled items') is present inside the Unscheduled complementary region.

feature_checks.feature: Unscheduled panel sort trigger (default 'Most recent, Descending')
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a button inside the Unscheduled panel whose accessible name starts with 'Most recent' and contains a 'Descending' icon.
feature_checks.notes: Menu contents were not enumerable via this agent's click path.

feature_checks.feature: Unscheduled panel has a Filters popup (Assignee, Priority, Status)
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Click the 'Filters' button inside the Unscheduled panel and assert the opened popup contains the section headings ASSIGNEE, PRIORITY, STATUS and the status options DONE, IN PROGRESS, TO DO.

feature_checks.feature: Unscheduled list renders work-item cards with key, summary, status, and priority
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert the Unscheduled list contains at least one link whose accessible name matches the fixture key (e.g. 'AUT-1'), and within that link the summary 'Design autonomous replanning loop', a 'Task' icon, the status 'To Do', and a priority icon (e.g. 'High priority').

feature_checks.feature: Dragging an unscheduled card onto a day cell sets that date as due date
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Presence-only: unscheduled list items have draggable affordances; the helper copy 'Drag each work item onto the calendar to set a due date for the work.' is visible. A full drag would mutate the item.
feature_checks.reverted_by: Open the item and clear the due date or drag back to Unscheduled panel.

feature_checks.feature: 'Enter full screen' button expands the calendar surface
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a button named 'Enter full screen' exists in the space header.

feature_checks.feature: Automation entry point from space header
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a button named 'Automation' exists in the space header.

feature_checks.feature: Global 'Create' button in the top banner for creating new work items
feature_checks.how_to_verify: Assert a button named 'Create' exists in the page banner.

known_gaps: Saturday/Sunday columns hidden by default ('Show weekends' off); assertions assume default settings.
The sort menu inside the Unscheduled panel did not expose menu contents via the automated click path — only the trigger button is asserted.
Drag-and-drop scheduling from Unscheduled to a calendar day is not executed (mutating).
Week view contents were not enumerated; only the toggle menu items Month/Week are captured.
No scheduled work items exist in the fixture period, so on-calendar event card layout is not asserted.

mutation_runbook.scenario: Create a work item on a specific day
steps: Hover a day gridcell (e.g. 'April 15, 2026') and click its 'Create work item' button.
Fill the inline summary and submit to create the work item with that due date.
Cleanup: open the created item and delete it (More actions → Delete) to restore the calendar.

mutation_runbook.scenario: Schedule an unscheduled item via drag-and-drop
steps: Drag the AUT-1 card onto a target day gridcell (e.g. 'April 23, 2026').
Cleanup: open the item's detail view and clear the Due date field (or drag it back to the Unscheduled panel).
Verify grid switches to a week layout.
Cleanup: click 'Month options' → 'Month' to restore default.
